title: Sinus symptoms
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Defines duration patterns and lists congestion, discharge, postnasal drip, dental discomfort, facial pain or pressure, fever, fatigue, cough, smell change, and headache.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped sinus terminology, duration, congestion, discharge, pressure, dental pain, fever, fatigue, cough, smell, headache, and assessed nasal or sinus fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines duration patterns and lists congestion, discharge, postnasal drip, dental discomfort, facial pain or pressure, fever, fatigue, cough, smell change, and headache.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis

red_flags.text: Lists high fever, frontal or periorbital swelling, severe headache, meningeal or mental-status change, visual change, and persistent unilateral or bloody symptoms.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped visual, swelling, severe headache, mental-status, fever, unilateral, and immunocompromise warning fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists high fever, frontal or periorbital swelling, severe headache, meningeal or mental-status change, visual change, and persistent unilateral or bloody symptoms.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis

red_flags.text: Limits imaging and other tests to clinician-selected contexts and lists emergency, specialist, systemic-illness, and non-response referral criteria.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-selected imaging, return, escalation, and follow-up documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Limits imaging and other tests to clinician-selected contexts and lists emergency, specialist, systemic-illness, and non-response referral criteria.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis

medication.text: Provides clinician-led conservative and pharmacological management choices; no dose-bearing content is mapped into this project.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented symptom care, medicine decisions, questions, and management discussions without doses.
medication.evidence_extract: Provides clinician-led conservative and pharmacological management choices; no dose-bearing content is mapped into this project.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Acute Rhinosinusitis

source_limitations: Throat and broad ear examination, prior-record review, fixed one-to-two-week timing, and diagnosis-specific medication details remain unsupported.
Sinusitis remains clinician-stated; individual purulence or facial pain must not be used alone to infer bacterial disease.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
